P1.title: Fix permuted sum precision issue for lower precision on CPU
P1.bug_pattern: Permuted bf16/fp16 CPU reductions truncate intermediate sums when TensorIterator reduces more than two logical dimensions.
P1.why_good: The added low-precision non-contiguous sum test compares against an explicit float reference, so base fails on accumulated truncation and the accumulator-buffer fix passes.

P1.title: [Inductor/Triton] Upcast FP16/BF16 math reductions to FP32
P1.bug_pattern: Generated Triton fp16/bf16 math reductions accumulate in low precision instead of promoting reducer inputs to fp32.
P1.why_good: The regression test compiles low-precision reductions and asserts the generated Triton contains fp32 casts while matching eager output.

P1.title: Fix rms_norm in fp16/bf16
P1.bug_pattern: RMSNorm downcasts the normalized value before the weight multiply, causing extra fp16/bf16 truncation.
P1.why_good: The numeric test compares functional RMSNorm with a reference that keeps the reduction and weight multiply in float until the final cast.

P1.title: fix layer_norm decomp precision for cpu
P1.bug_pattern: CPU native_layer_norm decomposition misses CPU-specific low-precision upcast/return-dtype behavior under fake tensor dispatch.
P1.why_good: The fake tensor decomposition test checks output dtype parity against real CPU bf16 native_layer_norm outputs.

P2.title: Set correct output dtype for dequantize op during convert_pt2e in decomposed mode
P2.bug_pattern: PT2E decomposed dequantize conversion drops the intended non-float output dtype such as bf16.
P2.why_good: The test drives convert_pt2e with non-float unquantized dtype and catches the wrong dequantized output dtype on the base commit.

P2.title: Update torch.masked.mean to upcast dtype for bool tensors
P2.bug_pattern: torch.masked.mean on bool tensors infers bool for the internal sum, clamping totals to True and losing count precision.
P2.why_good: The repro computes a bool masked mean where bool accumulation gives 0.5 instead of the integer-count mean; the patch upcasts the sum dtype.

P2.title: [Inductor] Unifiy Low Precision FP Legalization for to_dtype_bitcast & constant
P2.bug_pattern: Inductor low-precision bitcast legalization upcasts values before dtype-sensitive sink operations such as bitwise ops.
P2.why_good: The focused float16/bfloat16-to-int16 CPU tests fail when legalization inserts the wrong cast and pass once source/sink dtype propagation is unified.

P2.title: [MPS] Fix scalar to tensors bitshifts
P2.bug_pattern: MPS scalar-to-tensor bitshifts cast the scalar after the operation, diverging from CPU low-width integer dtype semantics.
P2.why_good: Regression tests compare uint8 scalar shift behavior and catch the output-value mismatch caused by delayed dtype conversion.

P2.title: Fix uint view copy
P2.bug_pattern: Inductor uint view-copy codegen undoes an upcast before dtype bitcast using imprecise dtype logic.
P2.why_good: The CUDA repro covers uint view copy through Triton codegen and fails when the generated dtype restoration is wrong.

P3.title: [inductor] fix linear_add_bias for autocast case
P3.bug_pattern: linear_add_bias fusion assumes bf16 bias under autocast and mishandles fp32 bias with bf16 weight.
P3.why_good: The autocast test exercises MKLDNN linear+bias fusion and catches the dtype mismatch that appears only under autocast.

P3.title: [NJT] manually autocast in SDPA handling
P3.bug_pattern: Nested jagged tensor SDPA intercepts before dispatcher autocast, so SDPA inputs are not cast to the expected autocast dtype.
P3.why_good: The nested tensor SDPA test runs with autocast enabled and fails unless sdpa.py manually applies the same casts as dispatcher autocast.

P3.title: [CUDA][AMP] Fix autocast_dtype
P3.bug_pattern: CUDA AMP autocast dtype is thread-local, causing backward side threads to use fp16 when forward selected bf16.
P3.why_good: The autocast test reproduces cross-thread dtype inconsistency and passes after autocast_dtype is made consistent across forward/backward threads.

P3.title: Fix autocast for non-strict export
P3.bug_pattern: Non-strict export drops autocast enter/exit nodes, losing mixed-precision context in the exported graph.
P3.why_good: The export_for_training test checks that autocast nodes remain in the non-strict export graph instead of silently disappearing.

P3.title: Fix autocast HOP pass for nested autocast
P3.bug_pattern: Autocast higher-order-op replacement pass mishandles nested autocast regions.
P3.why_good: The pass test covers nested autocast and fails if the transformed graph cannot preserve the nested autocast semantics.

P4.title: [PyTorch][Vulkan]fix the issue of log 0 after softmax
P4.bug_pattern: Vulkan log_softmax can take log(0) after fp16 softmax underflows tiny probabilities to zero, producing -inf/NaN.
P4.why_good: The Vulkan log_softmax tests exercise underflow-sized probabilities and catch non-finite output before the epsilon stabilization.

P4.title: Fix torch.clamp in MPS to handle NaN correctly
P4.bug_pattern: MPS torch.clamp uses min/max forms that do not propagate NaN like PyTorch clamp semantics require.
P4.why_good: The MPS clamp regression test checks NaN propagation directly and fails when NaN is clamped to a numeric bound.

P4.title: Fix exp decomp numerics
P4.bug_pattern: exp decomposition misses eager numeric guards, allowing sampled/log-transformed values to produce inf.
P4.why_good: The decomp test asserts non-inf behavior for the edge case described in the issue and passes once eager-style numeric handling is restored.

P4.title: Fix numerical instability for norm
P4.bug_pattern: Large CPU norm reductions overflow fp32 range when reduced directly, producing incorrect results.
P4.why_good: The reduction test covers a large norm case and fails on base until the implementation groups reductions and uses double intermediate accumulation.

P4.title: Avoid overflow in vector_norm for scalar input
P4.bug_pattern: vector_norm for scalar input computes power then root and can overflow where eager avoids the overflow.
P4.why_good: The Inductor/vector_norm test compares torch.dist/vector_norm scalar behavior against eager and catches the overflow-induced mismatch.

P5.title: [SymmetricMemoryOps] use float32 as the accumulator type when accumulating bfloat16 with multimem.ld_reduce
P5.bug_pattern: CUDA multimem.ld_reduce accumulates bfloat16 in bf16 precision instead of using fp32 accumulator precision.
P5.why_good: The distributed symmetric memory test sums random bf16 inputs across ranks and checks closeness to a gathered fp32-style reference, exercising the kernel accumulator change.

P5.title: Fix torch.histc not checking min > max on cuda for int8 tensors
P5.bug_pattern: CUDA histc stores min/max bounds in low-precision input_t for int8, making min > max checks compare the wrong values.
P5.why_good: The reductions test reproduces int8 CUDA histc with invalid bounds and fails until the kernel uses a wider bound type for the comparison.
